gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن أو موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/262)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن أو موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/262)
text: مُكَاتَباً أَتَى أَمِيرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) فَقَالَ إِنَّ سَيِّدِي كَاتَبَنِي وَ شَرَطَ عَلَيَّ نُجُوماً فِي كُلِّ سَنَةٍ فَجِئْتُهُ بِالْمَالِ كُلِّهِ ضَرْبَةً وَاحِدَةً وَ سَأَلْتُهُ أَنْ يَأْخُذَ كُلَّهُ ضَرْبَةً وَاحِدَةً وَ يُجِيزَ عِتْقِي فَأَبَى عَلَيَّ

فَدَعَاهُ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) فَقَالَ صَدَقَ فَقَالَ لَهُ مَا لَكَ لَا تَأْخُذُ الْمَالَ وَ تُمْضِيَ عِتْقَهُ فَقَالَ مَا آخُذُ إِلَّا النُّجُومَ الَّتِي شَرَطْتُ وَ أَتَعَرَّضُ مِنْ ذَلِكَ لِمِيرَاثِهِ فَقَالَ لَهُ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) فَأَنْتَ أَحَقُّ بِشَرْطِكَ .

en.hubeali: From him, from Al Hassan Bin Musa Al Khashab, from Giyas Bin Kaloub, from Is’haq Bin Ammar,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> (having said) that: ‘A contracted slave came to Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup>, so he said, ‘My master has contracted me and stipulated a condition upon me of installments during every year. So I came with all of the wealth, in one go, and asked him that he should take all of it in one go, and allow me my freedom. But he refused’.

So Amir Al Momineen<sup>asws</sup> called him over, so he said, ‘He speaks the truth’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said to him: ‘So what is the matter with you that you are not taking the wealth and fulfill his emancipation?’ So he said, ‘I will not take except by installments which I had stipulated, and I turn away from the due to his inheritance’. So Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘So you are more rightful of being upon your stipulation’.
en.sarwar: 2. It is narrated from the narrator of the previous Hadith from al-Hassan ibn Musa al-Khashshab from Ghiyath ibn Kalfub from Ishaq ibn ‘Ammar who has narrated the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that once a slave who had contracted his freedom came to ’ Amir al-Mu’minin, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, and said, ‘My master made a contract with me with the condition of several installment payments every year. I brought him all installments to pay at once and asked him to accept it and allow my freedom but he refused. Amir al-Mu’minin summoned him and he said, ‘He has spoken the truth.’ He (the Imam) asked, ‘Why do you not accept the assets and approve his freedom?’ He replied, ‘I accept only in installments as the condition requires and I avoid his legacy.’ Amir al-Mu’minin then said, ‘You have the right to stay with your condition.’”
